mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds 5.0% and remains elevated for 2+ consecutive months, the restrictive monetary stance suppresses equity valuations by raising the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings. This produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent 20 trading days.
regime_hint: monetary tightening cycle with restrictive rates above neutral
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 55% of instances when FEDFUNDS > 5.0% for 2+ months, or Rank IC < 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in total nonfarm payroll employment (PAYEMS) signals labor market deterioration and reduced consumer spending capacity. This mechanism operates continuously without threshold activation, producing negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days as earnings expectations compress.
failure_condition: Month-over-month PAYEMS declines do not precede negative 60-day SPX returns in > 50% of instances, or correlation between PAYEMS growth and 60-day forward returns is not significantly negative (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Rising real retail sales (RSAFS) combined with declining housing starts (HOUST) indicates a demand shift away from capital investment toward consumption. This interaction signals near-term equity strength as consumer spending drives earnings, but deteriorating future growth capacity. Positive 20-day returns expected.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS growth > 0.5% month-over-month AND HOUST declines > 5% month-over-month
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS at 673,902 million and HOUST at 1,584 thousand as of 2023-05-01
failure_condition: When both conditions are met, SPX produces negative 20-day returns in > 45% of instances, or the joint signal has lower predictive power than either component alone (Rank IC difference < 0.02).

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) declines below its 12-month moving average, signaling supply-side weakness and potential stagflation risk. This threshold-triggered mechanism produces negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days as profit margins compress under cost pressures.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO falls below its 12-month moving average
regime_hint: supply-constrained environment with rising input costs
failure_condition: INDPRO below 12-month MA does not precede negative 60-day returns in > 52% of instances, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Inflation (CPIAUCSL) accelerates above 4.0% year-over-year while the federal funds rate remains below the inflation rate, creating negative real rates. This interaction signals monetary accommodation that supports equity valuations through reduced real debt burdens and positive real asset returns over 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL YoY growth > 4.0% AND FEDFUNDS < CPIAUCSL YoY growth
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL at 304.014 and FEDFUNDS at 5.08% as of 2023-06-01
regime_hint: post-shock recovery with persistent inflation and accommodative real rates
failure_condition: When both conditions are met, SPX produces negative 20-day returns in > 50% of instances, or the interaction effect is not statistically significant (p > 0.10) in a multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: The unemployment rate (UNRATE) rises above 4.0%, signaling labor market slack and reduced wage pressure. This threshold-triggered mechanism produces positive S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days as markets price in lower future inflation and potential monetary easing.
regime_hint: late-cycle labor market softening with disinflation expectations
failure_condition: UNRATE > 4.0% does not precede positive 5-day returns in > 55% of instances, or the signal produces lower Rank IC than a random walk (IC < 0.02).

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth (GDPC1) decelerates to below 1.5% annualized rate, indicating late-cycle weakness and recession risk. This always-on mechanism produces negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days as earnings expectations contract in response to slowing economic activity.
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1 at 22,439.607 billion chained 2017 dollars as of 2023-01-01
failure_condition: Quarters with GDPC1 growth < 1.5% annualized do not precede negative 60-day SPX returns in > 52% of instances, or correlation between GDP growth and forward returns is not significantly positive (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) growth accelerates above 2.0% month-over-month while inflation (CPIAUCSL) remains stable below 3.5% YoY, signaling productivity gains without cost-push inflation. This interaction produces positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days as profit margins expand.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO growth > 2.0% month-over-month AND CPIAUCSL YoY growth < 3.5%
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO at 100.9286 and CPIAUCSL at 304.014 as of 2023-05-01 and 2023-06-01
failure_condition: When both conditions are met, SPX produces negative 20-day returns in > 48% of instances, or the joint signal has lower predictive power than INDPRO growth alone (Rank IC difference < 0.01).
